skill.name: firecrawl-search
skill.description: Search the web with Firecrawl and optionally extract full page content from search results.
skill.summary: Find web, image, news, GitHub, research, or PDF results and save Firecrawl output for analysis.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: The static findings for Ruby backtick execution, path traversal, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance are false positives from Markdown examples and links. The skill still carries medium risk because it authorizes Bash execution of Firecrawl commands, performs external web search and scraping, and writes output files.

medium_findings.title: External Firecrawl Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash execution of firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands. This is required for the skill, but it depends on an external CLI and should be limited to intended Firecrawl commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block directly authorizes Bash commands for firecrawl and npx firecrawl. The examples confirm intended external command use, but no malicious command is shown.

medium_findings.title: Network Search and Page Scraping
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to perform web search and optional full page scraping. This is legitimate functionality, but it sends search terms to an external service and may retrieve untrusted web content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The description and options explicitly state web search and scraping behavior. This confirms network activity, but it matches the declared purpose.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline option names, and usage examples. No Ruby code, dynamic evaluation, or hidden command substitution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible Markdown documentation around commands and options. There is no executable Ruby source or template expansion context in the file.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Link False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported path traversal sequences are relative Markdown links to neighboring skill documentation files. They are not file reads or writes performed by executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences appear only inside Markdown links under the See also section. No command or file operation consumes those paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance on the flagged lines. The cited lines contain frontmatter text, a table header, and output handling guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not name cryptographic algorithms or host reconnaissance commands. The scanner likely matched unrelated substrings in prose or Markdown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Execution for External CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill authorizes Bash commands for firecrawl and npx firecrawl. This pattern should remain tightly scoped because shell tools can affect the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata explicitly grants these Bash command patterns. The scope is narrow, but it is still external command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Selected Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples and options write search results to paths supplied through -o or --output. Misuse could overwrite files if a caller chooses an unsafe path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file output behavior is explicitly documented. The examples recommend .firecrawl paths, so the risk depends on caller behavior.

content.value_statement: Web research often needs fresh results and full page content, not only short snippets. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to search with Firecrawl and save structured results.
actual_capabilities: Runs Firecrawl search commands for web, image, and news sources.
Uses time filters for recent search results.
Can request full page scraping during search.
Supports category filters such as GitHub, research, and PDF.
Saves search output to local files for later analysis.
limitations: Requires the Firecrawl CLI or npx package to be available.
May require Firecrawl credentials or account configuration.
Search quality depends on Firecrawl service results.
Scraped pages can contain untrusted or outdated content.

use_cases.title: Source Discovery
use_cases.description: Find useful pages before starting a research summary or comparison.

use_cases.title: Recent News Monitoring
use_cases.description: Search news results from the past day, week, month, or year.

use_cases.title: Content Collection
use_cases.description: Search and fetch full page markdown in one Firecrawl request.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find general sources for a topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for reliable sources about renewable energy storage and save the results in .firecrawl.

prompt_templates.title: Recent News Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search recent news from the past day about the Federal Reserve decision and summarize the strongest sources.

prompt_templates.title: Research Category Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for research and PDF sources about retrieval augmented generation evaluation methods.

prompt_templates.title: Search with Full Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for articles about supply chain security frameworks, scrape the full content, and compare the most relevant results.

output_examples.input: Search for recent news about battery recycling policy.
output: A saved Firecrawl results file in the .firecrawl directory.
A ranked list of relevant news pages with titles and source links.
A short summary of the strongest matches and publication timing.

output_examples.input: Find research sources about AI code review benchmarks.
output: Search results filtered toward research and PDF sources.
A concise explanation of which sources appear most useful.
Suggested next steps for scraping or comparing selected pages.

best_practices: Write outputs to the .firecrawl directory to keep artifacts organized.
Use time filters when freshness matters.
Use scrape during search when full page content is needed.
anti_patterns: Do not scrape every result when snippets are enough.
Do not write output to sensitive or system paths.
Do not treat scraped web content as trusted without review.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the live web?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Firecrawl search commands to request live search results.

faq.question: Can it scrape full page content?
faq.answer: Yes. The scrape option requests page content during the search step.

faq.question: Where are results saved?
faq.answer: The skill recommends saving results inside the .firecrawl directory.

faq.question: Does it replace source review?
faq.answer: No. Users should still review source quality, dates, and reliability.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: It runs external Firecrawl commands, performs network requests, and writes local output files.
